exercises.prompt: “The argument continued ________, neither side willing to yield.” — Jonathan Swift, Gulliver's Travels

“From these premises, we may ________ the necessary conclusion.” — Jane Austen, Emma
“The ________ debates drew participants from every dormitory.” — F. Scott Fitzgerald, This Side of Paradise
“Someone whose anger cannot be ________d is, in adjective form, implacable.” — Alfred Lansing, Endurance
“‘Enough, too,’ ________ed George.” — Robert Louis Stevenson, Treasure Island
“The ________ ruled the family with quiet authority.” — Thomas Hardy, Tess of the d'Urbervilles
“His ________ pride was evident in every gesture.” — Charles Dickens, David Copperfield
“The most ________ city in the kingdom stretched along the river.” — Daniel Defoe, Robinson Crusoe

“himself lying on top of the ________ Rat .”

“She walked ________ly away across the grass as though trying to get rid of him .”
“out into the fog in a dignified and profoundly ________ attitude.”
“His ________ tone brooked no contradiction.” — Charlotte Brontë, Villette
“Music was not merely an ________ to the ceremony, but its soul.” — E.M. Forster, A Room with a View
“Permit me to ________ this obscure passage.” — Edgar Allan Poe, The Murders in the Rue Morgue

The ________ nature of the situation was evident to all.
The ________ nature of the situation was evident to all.

“Even when he was awake he was completely ________ .”
“I ________ entirely with your assessment of the situation.” — Arthur Conan Doyle, The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
“The ________ of routine proved unexpectedly liberating.” — Virginia Woolf, Mrs. Dalloway
“The ________ cruelty of the remark shocked us all.” — Emily Brontë, Wuthering Heights
“The ________ birds returned with the first warm winds.” — D.H. Lawrence, Sons and Lovers
“True art must ________ the limitations of its medium.” — Oscar Wilde, The Critic as Artist
The ________ nature of the situation was evident to all.
“His rocklike frame was motionless, his face ________, unsmiling, dark.”
“A sense of ________ doom hung over the household.” — Charles Dickens, Bleak House
“he was stricken early with a guilty fear of people and an ________ impulse to apologize to society for the fact that he was not Henry Fonda .”
“Your observations are not ________ to the matter at hand.” — William Shakespeare, Hamlet
“The ________ of new blood revitalized the old institution.” — George Eliot, Daniel Deronda
“He stood ________, uncertain how to respond.” — Charles Dickens, Great Expectations
“His injuries ________ any possibility of return.” — Ernest Hemingway, A Farewell to Arms
“She chose to ________ herself in the country.” — Jane Austen, Mansfield Park
The ________ nature of the situation was evident to all.
“Another year, two years, and they would be watching her night and day for symptoms of un________y .”

“you hear the July flies grinding away in the ________.”

“I do not ________ your motives, but question your methods.” — Anthony Trollope, The Way We Live Now
“His ________ manners concealed a ruthless ambition.” — F. Scott Fitzgerald, The Great Gatsby
“I could detect a curious shifting and shading of feelings on her face, too evanescent and ________ for definition.” — Robert Penn Warren
“this unmanageable mass of ________ bills, each an impediment to the other”

“The ________ mystery of existence haunted him.” — Joseph Conrad, Heart of Darkness

“I must ________ this error before it is too late.” — George Eliot, Silas Marner
“________ comes to all who violate nature’s laws.” — Nathaniel Hawthorne, The Scarlet Letter
“White Fang crawled slowly, cringing and groveling in the ________ness of his abasement and submission.”
“Naomi was a sister of England’s most clever and ________ biologist, J.B.S. Haldane.”

“Now she held up her hand, ________ly, and silenced Hagar’s whines.” — Toni Morrison
“His tender ________ for her welfare touched her deeply.” — Jane Austen, Mansfield Park
